2606.16883.title: Upper Bounds on the Generalization Error of Deep Learning Models via Local Robustness and Stability
2606.16883.paper_summary: Generalization is a critical property of data-driven models, particularly deep learning models deployed in safety-critical applications. Robustness-based generalization bounds have gained attention as a principled way to link robustness properties to generalization performance, often in a data-dependent manner. However, most existing bounds suffer from vacuousness in practical settings, yielding loose upper bounds that greatly exceed the actual error rates and limiting their usefulness for real-world evaluation. While this issue is often attributed to the uncertainty term, a substantial part of the problem originates from the robustness term itself, particularly for the 0-1 loss. Existing approaches typically treat the robustness term as a global measure, ignoring its variation across different sub-regions of the input space. In this work, we propose a generalization bound that addresses this limitation by scaling the robustness term according to the number of stable and unstable samples within each sub-region. Our bounds incorporate both data- and model-dependent factors while maintaining practical relevance (yielding tighter upper bounds on true error). Experiments on models trained on the ImageNet dataset show that our bounds remain consistently non-vacuous and achieve the tightest estimates among existing methods, closely aligning with empirical performance across a range of robust deep neural networks.
2606.16883.paper_summary_zh: 一般化是數據驅動模型的一個關鍵特性，特別是在安全關鍵應用中部署的深度學習模型。基於魯棒性的泛化界限因為能夠以原則性的方法將魯棒性特性與泛化性能聯繫起來而受到關注，通常是以數據依賴的方式進行。然而，大多數現有的界限在實際環境中存在空洞性，產生的上界遠遠超過實際錯誤率，限制了其在現實世界評估中的實用性。雖然這個問題通常被歸因於不確定性項，但問題的很大一部分實際上源自於魯棒性項本身，特別是對於0-1損失。現有的方法通常將魯棒性項視為全局度量，忽略了其在輸入空間不同子區域之間的變化。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個泛化界限，通過根據每個子區域內穩定和不穩定樣本的數量來縮放魯棒性項，以解決這一限制。我們的界限同時考慮了數據和模型依賴的因素，同時保持實用性（產生對真實錯誤的更緊湊的上界）。在ImageNet數據集上訓練的模型實驗顯示，我們的界限始終保持非空洞性，並在現有方法中實現了最緊湊的估計，與一系列魯棒深度神經網絡的實證性能密切對齊。
2606.16883.author: Abdul-Rauf Nuhu et.al.
2606.16883.authors: Abdul-Rauf Nuhu, Parham M. Kebria, Vahid Hemmati, Mahmoud N. Mahmoud, Edward Tunstel, Abdollah Homaifar
